source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in <image>, the conducting rod \( AB \) rotates in a uniform magnetic field around an axis \( OO' \) that passes through point \( C \), perpendicular to the length of the rod and along the direction of the magnetic field \( \omega \) (with \( \omega \) in the same direction as \( B \)). The length of \( BC \) is \( \frac{1}{3} \) of the length of the rod. Then:
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The electric potential at point \( A \) is higher than that at point \( B \).

choice_map.A: The electric potential at point \( A \) is lower than that at point \( B \).
choice_map.B: The electric potential at point \( A \) is equal to that at point \( B \).
choice_map.C: The electric potential at point \( A \) is higher than that at point \( B \).
choice_map.D: There is a current flowing from point \( A \) to point \( B \).

multi_step: The question combines several conditions: rotation axis through C, axis perpendicular to the rod, axis along the magnetic field direction, angular velocity direction relative to B, and BC being 1/3 of the rod length
Answering requires integrating diagram interpretation with physics reasoning about motional emf/potential distribution along different parts of the rod
The target is a comparative conclusion about potentials at A and B rather than direct fact lookup
